title: senecio neoviscosus
intro: Senecio neoviscosus, also known as New Zealand fireweed or sticky fireweed, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand and Australia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and sticky leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Senecio neoviscosus include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular herbal remedy for skin issues and infections. The plant is typically prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, and can also be used externally as a topical application to take advantage of its medicinal and health benefits.
